commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The blocker is a maintainer policy decision for group verbose privacy and upgrade compatibility, not a safe automated repair on a draft PR.; Needs attention: The patch changes a group disclosure boundary and currently lets inherited verbose defaults expose tool/progress summaries in normal groups.; Review finding: [P1] Require explicit group opt-in before inherited verbose defaults — src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-fr... (sha=39562f6bd7d66f3fa2053d59e76697b05a5b4084)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes shared reply dispatch, ACP projection, tests, and group docs so normal group sessions can emit verbose tool/progress summaries when verbose is enabled.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main's source gate suppresses normal group tool summaries, and the PR body supplies a redacted live Telegram before/after transcript; I did not rerun live Telegram in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The blocker is a maintainer policy decision for group verbose privacy and upgrade compatibility, not a safe automated repair on a draft PR.

target.title: fix(channels): honor /verbose in group sessions
target.body:
## Summary

AI-assisted with Codex; I reviewed the diff, ran live Telegram proof for the user-visible behavior, fixed local Codex review findings, and ran focused tests on the current head.

- Problem: normal group sessions suppress text-only tool/progress summaries even when `/verbose on` is enabled.
- Solution: make the shared group dispatch gate honor the existing verbose session state instead of adding a channel-specific flag.
- What changed: group sessions stay quiet by default, but `/verbose on` now allows tool/progress summaries through the shared dispatch and ACP projection paths; `/verbose off` can hide them again during a run.
- What did NOT change: command/tool execution behavior, secrets, permissions, Slack non-DM suppression, and forum-topic behavior are unchanged.

## Motivation

Private group QA is useful for testing real OpenClaw behavior, but without visible tool/progress summaries it is hard to verify what the agent actually did. `/verbose` already controls this visibility, so group dispatch should respect that existing session state instead of silently suppressing summaries forever.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  Normal group sessions did not show text-only tool/progress summaries after `/verbose on`.

- Real environment tested:
  Private Telegram QA group with two BotFather bots:
  - Driver bot sends prompts into the group.
  - SUT bot runs OpenClaw gateway from this PR branch.
  - Normal Telegram supergroup, not a forum topic.
  - Tokens, chat IDs, and private config are omitted.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Start QA SUT gateway from this PR branch.
  2. Send `/verbose@qa_sut_bot off`.
  3. Prompt SUT: `Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-fix-verbose-off-done.`
  4. Send `/verbose@qa_sut_bot on`.
  5. Prompt SUT: `Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-fix-verbose-on-done.`

- Evidence after fix (live Telegram output):

  ```text
  /verbose@qa_sut_bot off
  QA SUT: ⚙️ Verbose logging disabled.

  @qa_sut_bot Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-fix-verbose-off-done.
  QA SUT: qa-fix-verbose-off-done

  /verbose@qa_sut_bot on
  QA SUT: ⚙️ Verbose logging enabled.

  @qa_sut_bot Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-fix-verbose-on-done.
  QA SUT: 🛠️ pwd
  QA SUT: qa-fix-verbose-on-done
  ```

  Screenshot evidence was attached in the PR discussion/body showing QA Driver prompting QA SUT, QA SUT emitting a visible tool summary, and QA SUT returning the requested result.

- Observed result after fix:
  `/verbose off` keeps group tool summaries hidden. `/verbose on` shows the tool summary before the final reply.

- What was not tested:
  I did not run the full repository test suite on this low-memory VPS. I do not have live Slack/Matrix/WhatsApp bots configured for proof, so non-Telegram channels are covered by focused shared-dispatch tests rather than live channel proof.

- Before evidence:
  Copied live Telegram transcript from `origin/main`:

  ```text
  /verbose@qa_sut_bot off
  QA SUT: ⚙️ Verbose logging disabled.

  @qa_sut_bot Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-main-verbose-off-done.
  QA SUT: qa-main-verbose-off-done

  /verbose@qa_sut_bot on
  QA SUT: ⚙️ Verbose logging enabled.

  @qa_sut_bot Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-main-verbose-on-done.
  QA SUT: qa-main-verbose-on-done
  ```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause:
  Shared group dispatch had a broad normal-group suppression path for text-only tool/progress summaries, but that path did not account for the existing `/verbose` session state.

- Missing detection / guardrail:
  Existing tests covered default group suppression, forum-topic delivery, and Slack non-DM suppression, but not `/verbose on` enabling normal group summaries through the shared dispatch path.

- Contributing context:
  Telegram QA exposed the bug, but the fixed gate is intentionally shared across group-capable channels.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient

- Target test or file:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts`
  - `src/plugin-sdk/acp-runtime.test.ts`

- Scenario the test should lock in:
  Groups suppress tool summaries by default, allow them when existing verbose state is enabled, continue respecting live `/verbose` changes during long ACP turns, preserve hidden-tool text boundaries, and keep Slack non-DM/forum-topic behavior unchanged.

- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
  The bug is in shared dispatch/projection gates, so focused dispatch and ACP projector tests cover it without needing live credentials for every channel.

- Existing test that already covers this (if any):
  Existing tests covered group suppression and forum-topic delivery, but not normal group delivery controlled by verbose state.

- If no new test is added, why not:
  N/A. New focused tests are included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`/verbose on` now shows tool/progress summaries in normal group sessions. `/verbose off` keeps those summaries hidden.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
group + /verbose on -> tool summary suppressed -> final reply only

After:
group + /verbose off -> final reply only
group + /verbose on  -> tool summary -> final reply
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No execution behavior changed; existing verbose tool/progress summaries become visible in group sessions only when `/verbose on` is active.
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  Risk: visible tool/progress summaries can expose command names or arguments to group members. Mitigation: group sessions remain quiet by default with `/verbose off`; users must opt in with `/verbose on` for that group session, and `/verbose off` hides queued ACP summaries before final delivery.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux VPS
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw gateway running from this PR branch
- Model/provider: existing QA SUT config
- Integration/channel: Telegram normal group
- Relevant config (redacted): private QA Telegram group with Driver bot and SUT bot; tokens and chat IDs omitted

### Steps

1. Start the QA SUT gateway from this PR branch.
2. In the QA Telegram group, send `/verbose@qa_sut_bot off`.
3. Prompt the SUT to run `pwd` and reply with a marker.
4. Confirm only the final marker appears.
5. Send `/verbose@qa_sut_bot on`.
6. Prompt the SUT to run `pwd` and reply with a marker.
7. Confirm Telegram shows `🛠️ pwd`, then the final marker.

### Expected

Group tool/progress summaries are hidden with `/verbose off` and visible with `/verbose on`.

### Actual

Matches expected in Telegram QA proof.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Local checks on current head `39562f6bd7`:
  - `git diff --check`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/acp-runtime-backend.ts src/plugin-sdk/acp-runtime.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts src/plugins/hook-types.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts` -> `26 passed`
  - `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/acp-runtime.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t "tool-summary predicate|group tool summaries|Slack non-DM verbose progress|forum topic|refreshes verbose progress"` -> `9 passed | 141 skipped`

- Local Codex review:
  - `codex review --base origin/main` found three actionable P2 issues during iteration; all were fixed in this branch:
    - ACP dispatch must receive a live verbose predicate, not a one-time boolean.
    - ACP `final_only` buffered summaries must re-check the live gate before flush.
    - Dynamically hidden ACP tool events must still preserve hidden-boundary spacing.
  - Final rerun was attempted at 2026-05-22 21:37 UTC but was blocked by the local Codex quota window: `try again at 10:08 PM`.

- Verified scenarios:
  - `origin/main`: `/verbose on` still produced final reply only in a normal Telegram group.
  - This PR path: `/verbose off` produced final reply only.
  - This PR path: `/verbose on` produced visible `🛠️ pwd` tool summary, then final reply.

- Edge cases checked:
  - Existing forum-topic behavior remains covered by focused dispatch tests.
  - Slack non-DM suppression remains unchanged by the dispatch condition.
  - ACP final-only buffering re-checks the dynamic verbose gate before flushing.
  - Dynamically hidden ACP tool events preserve visible text spacing.

- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full repository test suite.
  - Live Slack/Matrix/WhatsApp behavior.
  - Final local Codex review rerun after the last fix, due quota limit noted above.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `/verbose on` in a group can expose tool/progress summary text to group members.
  - Mitigation: `/verbose off` remains the default quiet behavior for groups, this PR does not add a new always-on config flag, and ACP re-checks the live verbose gate before flushing buffered summaries.
